insight: Scorchilla at 2 gold + Green + Red + Red tech cost is considered too spammable and self-synergizing at a 2-tech level. Its burst damage self-synergizes too well, and when combined with other strong units like Hellhound and Feral in the same set, whoever starts the Scorchilla train simply wins.
author: spiritfryer
context: Discussion of a proposed Scorchilla cost reduction to 2GRR and whether that would be balanced.

insight: Deadeye Operative cannot snipe a blocking Drone, but a chilled Drone is no longer blocking and therefore becomes a valid snipe target. This creates a niche interaction where Cryo Ray can chill a Drone that an opponent intended to use as a blocker (e.g., to Forcefield next turn), enabling Deadeye to snipe it and potentially win the game.
author: velizar_
context: Discussion of Deadeye Operative mechanics and proposed redesigns, including the interaction between chilling and sniping.

insight: Chilled Drones cannot be sniped by Deadeye Operative when they are in blocking position, but can be sniped once chilled out of blocking. Additionally, exhausted Drones (via Dgrid) cannot be Forcefielded but can still be sniped by Deadeye.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 was investigating which Drone states are immune to Deadeye sniping, noting that Dgrid-exhausted Drones are especially vulnerable.

insight: Lancetooth is considered already weak compared to Bloodrager in a vacuum, but has compensating advantages: its BT2 allows purchasing more units than Bloodrager, it does not require an Animus so it better defends against pressure, and it has a better gold-to-tech ratio. Nerfing it to 8 gold + Blue is considered too harsh by some experts, though masn6811 argues it is still very strong at that cost due to its interaction with Steelsplitter (making Walls ineffective) and its problematic synergies with Borehole Patroller and Drake.
author: consensus
context: Community debate about whether Lancetooth deserved a nerf, with chloe1price, megasupp, shadourow, and masn6811 all contributing perspectives.

insight: Scorchilla is nearly as efficient as Tarsier offensively but counters large absorbers through self-synergy, counters frontline units and lifespan defenders because it can be held, counters red rush because it is breachproof, and counters greedy economies because it is cheap. Its key drawback is the supply limit of 10: once exhausted, you must switch to Tarsiers, which are easily absorbed and destroy your breachproof status.
author: chloe1price
context: Analysis of Scorchilla's strategic strengths and weaknesses in response to a balance discussion.

insight: With Vivid Drone in the set, the DEE (Drone-Engineer-Engineer) opening on turn 1 as P1 is considered a serious mistake because it prevents buying four Drones (DDDD) on turn 3, which is a key tempo play. masn6811 and silentslayers note that a disturbing number of 1800 Elo players make this error, possibly due to campaign conditioning where DEE is correct with Gilded Drone (the old Vivid Drone).
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 and silentslayers discussing opening theory with Vivid Drone sets and why the DEE line is incorrect.

insight: In high-level play, a non-trivial number of games are effectively decided on turn 1, particularly in sets with alternate drones. masn6811 and silentslayers agree that while the conventional wisdom is that most sub-1900 games are decided in the first 4 turns, early-decision sets can lock in win/loss even at 2100+ Elo.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of early-game decision importance in Prismata, specifically in the context of alternate drone sets.

insight: A colorless shock (Gauss Charge costing only gold, no Green) at 2 gold is considered completely broken by masn6811 and silentslayers. The key insight is that the first two Gauss Charges kill Engineers, then subsequent ones kill Drones, and colorless access means both players can spam them from turn 1 with no tech cost, creating degenerate races. A cost of 2.5 gold might be the balance point, but 2 gold colorless is fundamentally too efficient.
author: masn6811
context: Analysis of a hypothetical colorless Gauss Charge at 2 gold cost, triggered by a fan-made unit design discussion.

insight: A Magne Splitter-style unit that converts Walls into Pixies is problematic when it has the Blocker property, because clicking it costs double tempo: the unit stops blocking (losing 4 defense tempo) while simultaneously converting an opponent's Wall into Pixies (generating attack against yourself). Multiple experts agreed this makes the Blocker property specifically toxic on such a unit.
author: velizar_
context: Design discussion of a fan-made Magne Splitter unit with a Wall-to-Pixies conversion ability, evaluating whether it should have the Blocker property.
